applied_optimizations.name: CUDA Graph capture
applied_optimizations.description: Wrapped GRU forward pass in torch.cuda.CUDAGraph to eliminate kernel launch overhead. GRU has many sequential kernel launches per timestep/layer/direction, and CUDA graph captures and replays them efficiently.

attempted_but_failed.name: Manual Triton GRU cell
attempted_but_failed.reason: Correctness issues with multi-layer bidirectional GRU - very error-prone to match PyTorch exactly

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile on nn.GRU
attempted_but_failed.reason: Graph break - torch.compile doesn't support RNN/GRU/LSTM modules

attempted_but_failed.name: cudnn.benchmark=True
attempted_but_failed.reason: Performance regression - made implementation slower than baseline

notes: CUDA Graph is extremely effective for GRU - eliminates kernel launch overhead for 512 timesteps x 6 layers x 2 directions = 6144 kernel launches
Variance dropped from std ~30ms to std ~0.02ms with CUDA graph
MIOpen-optimized nn.GRU wrapped with CUDA graph beats manual Triton attempts

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Three consecutive trials (2, 3, 4) produced no output. The agent is stuck and only ~24 minutes remain. The best result is score 72.60 achieved in trial 1 via CUDA Graph capture, which reduced GRU runt
